title: Sarn
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:AdmiralSarn.jpg]] |name=Sarn |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ''[[Terror]]'', [[Imdaar Alpha]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Gray |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|In twelve hours, a fleet will stand ready at Imdaar Alpha where they will be poised to lay waste to the Rebels.|Admiral Sarn, to Darth Vader|audio=Sarn-RAII.flac|}} '''Sarn''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] who commanded the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Terror]]'' around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. The ''Terror'' was requisitioned by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Martio Batch]] to guard the development of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] new [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]]—a project Sarn hoped would help him to win [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] favor. During an inspection by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], two [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents, [[Rookie One]] and [[Ru Murleen]], [[Mission to Imdaar|infiltrated]] the ''Terror'' and destroyed it along with the [[Imdaar Alpha research station|TIE Phantom production facility]] on [[Imdaar Alpha]]. Vader escaped the ''Terror'' but [[Death/Legends|executed]] Sarn for his failure before leaving. ==Biography== ===TIE Phantom project=== {{Quote|As you can see, Lord Vader, the units perform admirably. In fact, I think the whole project has proved quite impressive. Why, in a few years…''"
"''What I see, Admiral Sarn, is yet another one of your endless tests. I will not be impressed until I have, under my command, the full power to crush the Rebel Alliance.|Sarn and Darth Vader|audio=SarnVader-RAII.flac|}} [[File:SarnVader.png|thumb|left|200px|Sarn reports to Darth Vader.]] The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Sarn served as an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Sarn had reached the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] and had been given command of the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Terror]]''. At his [[Imdaar Alpha research station|research station]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Imdaar Alpha]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Martio Batch]] was working to develop a practical [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]]. The devices had once been common, based around [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] [[Mining/Legends|mined]] from [[Aeten II/Legends|Aeten II]] in the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]], but became rare after the mines ran dry. After a failed attempt to produce cloaking devices based on [[Hibridium/Legends|hibridium]], Batch used the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] to [[Destruction of Aeten II|destroy]] Aeten II, an explosion that released thousands of stygium crystals into [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The Grand Admiral used the new supply of stygium to begin production of cloaked [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}—modified [[V38 assault fighter]]s known as [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantoms]]. To protect his project, he had Sarn and the ''Terror'' transferred to Imdaar Alpha. Sarn saw his involvement in the project as an opportunity to win favor with the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]].{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} Following the Imperial victory over the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in 3 ABY, the Empire began testing the new [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] Phantoms in the region of space known as the [[Dreighton Triangle]], using reports of unexplained events in the region ever since the [[Battle of Dreighton]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as a cover for its operations. When the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] traveled to Imdaar Alpha to inspect the progress of the project, Sarn arranged a demonstration of the new fighters, dispatching [[Sigma Squadron (TIE/ph Phantom)|Sigma Squadron]] to intercept a [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] group of Rebel [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] as they patrolled the Dreighton Triangle and instructing his crew to ensure that it proceed flawlessly. Observing aboard the ''Terror'', Sarn was impressed with the performance of the fighters, which destroyed all three Rebel [[Starship/Legends|ships]] before they could respond. Vader, however, was impatient to end the testing stage and turn the TIE Phantoms loose on the Rebel Alliance. ===Death and legacy=== {{Quote|You have failed me for the last time. You will not have an opportunity to do so again.|Darth Vader executes Sarn|audio=Vader-RAII.flac|}} [[File:SarnExecuted.png|thumb|right|200px|Sarn is executed by Darth Vader.]] Vader remained aboard the ''Terror'' while the field tests were completed. Sarn informed him that the TIE Phantom [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was ready for full deployment, and was ordered to take the ''Terror'' back to Imdaar Alpha. When the Rebel Alliance learned that the ''Terror'' was en route to [[Imdaar/Legends|Imdaar]], it dispatched [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] [[Ru Murleen]] and [[Rookie One]] to [[Mission to Imdaar|infiltrate]] the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] and steal a TIE Phantom for study. Infiltrating the [[Imdaar landing platform|supply platform]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], the Rebel agents stole a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class supply shuttle]] and used it get aboard the ''Terror'' as the ship prepared to dock with the cloaked TIE Phantom factory. The intruders were soon discovered but successfully evaded capture. On Vader's orders, Sarn doubled the search patrols in an effort to locate the Rebels, but the interlopers were able to reach the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and board a TIE Phantom. The Rebels used their stolen fighter to attack the ''Terror''{{'s}} [[Reactor/Legends|main reactor]], causing it to overload before making their escape. Sarn's crew attempted in vain to stop the reactor from going critical while Vader ordered Sarn to undock the ''Terror'' from the TIE Phantom factory. When Sarn reported that docking controls were inoperative, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Death/Legends|executed]] him for his failure, using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically strangle]] the admiral. Vader escaped the ''Terror'' moments before its destruction. As the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] exploded, it caused the TIE Phantom factory to become visible. The Rebels flew their stolen fighter inside and destroyed the facility, which brought an end to the TIE Phantom project. Knowing that he had failed the Emperor, Grand Admiral Batch took his fleet and fled to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. However, his crew was still loyal to Sarn. Following the Emperor's death at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Batch's crew [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] the Grand Admiral and joined [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Blitzer Harrsk]]'s [[Zero Command|splinter faction]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. ==Personality and traits== Sarn was an ambitious and conceited [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who saw his involvement in the TIE Phantom project as an opportunity to win the favor of Emperor Palpatine. He regarded the [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] possessed by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader as mysterious. Fearful of Vader's abilities, Sarn was careful not to anger the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]]. When Vader was sent to inspect the project's progress, Sarn was determined to ensure a flawless demonstration. Despite Sarn's efforts, the project was ultimately sabotaged by Rebel agents, and Vader used his powers to execute the admiral. ==Behind the scenes== Admiral Sarn was created by [[Vincent Lee]] for the [[1995]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]''. The game's story was told through a series of live-action cutscenes, in which Sarn was portrayed by [[Gary Martinez]]. As part of the game's promotional material, a brief description of the character appeared on the LucasArts website. [[Rob Smith]]'s ''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' includes an extract from Lee's storyboards and scripts document for ''Rebel Assault II'', dated [[November 16]], [[1994]], which shows Sarn in an early draft of the game's opening scene.''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' Sarn was mentioned in Grand Admiral Batch's entry in [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Daniel Wallace]]'s [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]] article, "[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]." The article was originally due to be published in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' before the magazine was cancelled and some material was cut when the article was later picked up by ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''.{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}} The original manuscript, entitled "[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]," established that Sarn was a former protégé of then-admiral Harrsk and that the ''Terror'' had been part of a task force requisitioned by Batch to protect the Imdaar Alpha facility."[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]" Admiral Sarn was one of the random names used by [[Wikipedia:Artificial intelligence in video games|computer-controlled]] Imperial players in the [[2001]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Sarn|imagecat=Images of Sarn}} *{{GalaxyCite|5|Game Room|Game Room: Rebel Rouser}} *''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen, Revised]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II Official Player's Guide]]'' *{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} *{{GalaxyCite|11|Game Room|Game Room: Darth Vader's Plenty Game}} {{Po}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' *{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' *''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' *{{CSWECite|III||Terror}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Sarn }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy admirals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
